# SoCal Tech Week 2024

### This year’s theme, “For the Audience,” challenges you to build impactful tools tailored to real-world communities. Utilize your experiences to help bring their ideas to life.

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://socal-tech-week-2024.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Ages 18+ only
* College students only
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://socal-tech-week-2024.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Nov 9 – 10, 2024**

|[Ronald Tutor Campus Center](https://maps.google.com/?q=3607 Trousdale Pkwy, Los Angeles, CA 90089, USA) |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 48,050** in prizes |**183** participants |

[HackSC](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=HackSC)

[Beginner Friendly](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Beginner+Friendly) [Design](https://devpost.com/hackathons?
themes%5B%5D=Design) [Social Good](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Social+Good)

Located in sunny Southern California at USC's campus, SoCal Tech Week is a 24-hour hackathon that combines innovation, creativity, and education. This year’s theme, “For the Audience,” challenges you to build purposeful, impactful tools tailored to real-world communities that are often overlooked in tech. Whether you're a team of beginners or experienced coders, utilize your experiences to help bring their ideas to life.

### Requirements

Please submit the Google form found in the Hacker Packet by 1pm. Submit your project on Devpost. This is due at 2:00 pm. Late submissions will not be accepted. The submission form is not short, so do not start a minute before the deadline.

### Hackathon Sponsors

##### Supported By

[](https://www.marshall.usc.edu/institutes-and-centers/vaneck-digital-assets-initiative "Go to USC VanEck Digital Assests page")

##### Partners

[](https://dain.
org/ "Go to DAIN page") [](https://www.capitalgroup.com/ "Go to Capital Group page") [](https://hellohelium.com/ "Go to Helium Mobile page") [](https://nosana.io/ "Go to Nosana page") [](https://moonwalk.fit/ "Go to Moonwalk page") [](https://www.anyone.io/ "Go to Anyone page") [](https://www.solanau.org/ "Go to SolanaU page") [](https://turbin3.com/ "Go to Turbin3 page")

### Prizes

$ 48,050 in prizes

###### For Accessibility

2 winners

\- 4x $200 Amex Giftcards  
\- Capital Group Merch

###### For The Artists

1 winner

\- Sony Headphones  
\- Numark DJ Set  
\- Lego Architecture Set (Himeji)  
\- Sony Bluetooth Stereo Bluetooth Record Player

###### For The Adventurers

1 winner

\- 2x Patagonia Backpacks (30L)  
\- 2x Patagonia Duffel Bags (70L)

###### For the Athletes

1 winner

\- 4x Apple watches

###### Battlepass Winner

3 winners

\- 1st place: $50 Amazon Giftcard  
\- 2nd place: $25 Amazon Giftcard  
\- 3rd place: $10 Amazon Giftcard

###### DAIN Sponsor Challenge

3 winners

results.title: Global AI Agents League: A Nexus of AI Agents - Devpost
excerpts:
# Global AI Agents League

### A Nexus of AI Agents

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://fetch-ai-hackathon.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Above legal age of majority in country of residence
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://fetch-ai-hackathon.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Feb 14 – Apr 8, 2025**

|Online |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 10,000** in cash |**1460** participants |

[Fetch.ai](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=Fetch.ai)

[Blockchain](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Blockchain) [Fintech](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Fintech) [Machine Learning/AI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Machine+Learning%2FAI)

**About the challenge**
Unleash your creativity by designing specialised AI Agents in any domain—whether it's Finance, Healthcare, Education, Quantum or Social Impact. Using any Agentic framework of your choice, register your agents on Agentverse, a dynamic central directory where agents seamlessly interact and collaborate to deliver powerful solutions.

Take it a step further by building a personalized assistant that leverages the Search and Discovery feature on [**Agentverse**](https://agentverse.ai/) . Your assistant will intelligently connect with other agents to fulfil user needs, orchestrating tasks with precision and efficiency.

Picture a world where users can effortlessly engage with a network of AI Agents tailored to their unique requirements—this is your opportunity to make it a reality.

Are you ready to innovate, collaborate, and automate the future of intelligent systems? The challenge awaits!

##### Why Join

* Be part of the Fetch.ai developer community.
* Build some innovative AI Agents using different frameworks.
* Get a chance to win some amazing prizes.

##### Getting Started

* Register for the hackathon
* Register on [Agentverse](https://agentverse.ai/) .
* Check out our [Resources](https://innovationlab.fetch.ai/resources/docs/intro) page
* Start brainstorming your ideas
* Complete verification through the [\](https://discord.gg/HfSmqqBMAM) channel, wait for a few minutes and click [here](https://discord.gg/3zQv34a3xY) to join the [\](https://discord.gg/3zQv34a3xY) channel to start posting your queries.
* Check out the [Hackpack](https://fetch.ai/events/global-ai-agents-league)

### Requirements

#### What to Build

In this hackathon, participants are encouraged to showcase their skills by building innovative solutions centered around AI Agents. Here's what you'll create:

#### 1\. Specialized AI Agents

results.title: SB Hacks XI: Make Waves at UCSB's 11th Annual Hackathon - Devpost
excerpts:
# SB Hacks XI

### Make Waves at UCSB's 11th Annual Hackathon

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://sb-hacks-xi.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Above legal age of majority in country of residence
* College students only
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://sb-hacks-xi.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Jan 11 – 12, 2025**

|[Corwin Pavilion](https://maps.google.com/?q=494%20UCEN%20Rd%2C%20Isla%20Vista%2C%20CA%2093117%2C%20USA) |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**$ 7,515** in prizes |**220** participants |

[SB Hacks](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=SB+Hacks)

[Beginner Friendly](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Beginner+Friendly) [Education](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Education) [Machine Learning/AI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Machine+Learning%2FAI)
Learn, create, and develop exciting projects and connections at UCSB’s 11th annual hackathon! Mark your calendars for January 11-12th for a weekend of building real-world solutions, competing for incredible prizes, and networking with the coolest hackers, mentors, and sponsors. Our team is excited to welcome hundreds of participants of all skill sets and backgrounds to the stunning UC Santa Barbara campus. Whether you are a first-time coder or a seasoned hacker, we encourage you to enter SB Hacks XI with enthusiasm and curiosity. We hope that you challenge yourself with a fun project, learn something new, and feel proud of what you accomplished at the end of it all.

What you can look forward to:

* A 24-hour adrenaline rush from problem-solving, coding, and collaborating
* Hands-on experience using company tech and hardware not accessible elsewhere
* Personalized mentorship by industry professionals
* Vast array of workshops to develop your skill set
* Free food, snacks, and drinks all weekend (one less adult responsibility to worry about)
* $100 worth of API credits from Anthropic for each hackathon participant
* Experiencing the beautiful Santa Barbara sunsets, beaches, and weather!

Visit our website [www.sbhacks.com](http://www.sbhacks.com) for more information!

### Requirements

All projects must be submitted to Devpost by 8:30 AM Sunday, Jan. 12. Teams must present their project live in front of judges on Sunday morning.

Judging will last from 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM on Sunday, Jan 12. Anyone who plans to be present during their live demos must be ready by 9:00 AM.

The time limit for all demos is 2.5 minutes. For live demonstrations, judges will have 1.5 minutes to ask any questions after the demonstration is over. Make sure that your presentation shows off your project in a clear and concise manner.

excerpts:
com/forum_topics)

# AI for Bharat 2026

### Year in Review, Code for Tomorrow

**This hackathon has ended**

[Find more hackathons](https://devpost.com/hackathons) [View the winners](https://ai-for-bharat-2026.devpost.com/project-gallery)

###### Who can participate

* Ages 13+ only
* College students only
* All countries/territories, excluding standard exceptions

View full rules

[View schedule](https://ai-for-bharat-2026.devpost.com/details/dates)

**Dec 29, 2025 – Jan 30, 2026**

|Online |Public |
| --- | --- |
|**3** non-cash prizes |**869** participants |

[student](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=student)

[Education](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Education) [Lifehacks](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Lifehacks) [Open Ended](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Open+Ended)

**Theme: “AI Solutions for Everyday Student Life in Bharat”**

**Theme Description (Use on Devpost):**
AI for Bharat 2025 is an online hackathon focused on building **AI-powered solutions that solve real problems faced by college students in their daily academic and personal lives** .

Participants will design and develop innovative tools using **Artificial Intelligence** to improve:

* Learning & productivity
* Career preparation
* Mental well-being
* Campus life & accessibility
* Financial & time management

The goal is to create **practical, affordable, and India-first AI solutions** that can genuinely help students across Bharat.

### Requirements

🔥 Project Themes / Tracks (Very Popular for 2025)

Participants can build projects under **any one** of the following themes:

1️⃣ **AI for Learning & Academics** 📚

Solve day-to-day academic challenges.

**Example ideas:**

* AI study planner & timetable generator
* AI notes summarizer from PDFs/videos
* Assignment & exam prep assistant
* Doubt-solving chatbot (multi-language)

2️⃣ **AI for Career & Placements** 🎓💼
Help students become job-ready.

**Example ideas:**

* AI resume & ATS score checker
* Mock interview AI (voice/text)
* Skill gap analyzer for students
* Internship & job recommendation system

3️⃣ **AI for Productivity & Time Management** ⏰

Reduce stress and improve focus.

**Example ideas:**

* AI task prioritization app
* Smart attendance & reminder system
* AI distraction blocker
* Daily routine optimizer

4️⃣ **AI for Mental Health & Wellness** 🧠

Student well-being is a big 2025 focus.

**Example ideas:**

* AI mood tracker & wellness chatbot
* Stress detection via text analysis
* Anonymous AI support assistant
* Meditation & habit-building AI tools

5️⃣ **AI for Campus Life & Accessibility** 🏫

Make college life easier.

**Example ideas:**

* Campus navigation AI
* AI complaint & feedback system
* Event & club recommendation AI
* Tools for differently-abled students

6️⃣ **AI for Bharat (Open Innovation)** 🇮🇳

Student problems with an Indian context.

results.title: AI ATL 2025: Atlanta's Premier Generative AI Hackathon - Devpost
excerpts:
##### For every project that is being created and submitted for AI ATL 2025, we require a Devpost submission. The submission will be used to make decision on winners in addition to the live demos on the final day of the hackathon, so please be sure to put effort into your submission whether that be your video, description, etc....  
  
To be eligible for tracks and challenges for your submission, you must select the specific prize categories that you are eligible for (e.g. Google, PrizePicks, Drive Capital, etc...).  
  
You must also submit a Github repository along with your submission that was created BETWEEN when hacking official began and when hacking ends.  
  
For the devpost submission, we are requiring a recorded demo video. This is generally up to the team's discretion, but it is recommend to be roughly 5 minutes long and show a demonstration of your project including features, functionality, and introduction to the problem space.
Your Devpost submission must be made no matter than 12AM (midnight) on Sunday November 9th, 2025. It is recommended that you publish your project before then and all members of the team must be added to the submission. Additionally, your Github can not be edited after 8 AM Sunday November 9th, 2025. We will NOT accept any submissions made after the submission deadline. You can publish your project any time during the weekend and still edit the submission before the deadline, and it is recommended to all participants that you do.

### Hackathon Sponsors

[](https://mlh.io/ "Go to Major League Hacking page")

### Prizes

15 non-cash prizes

###### Overall Track Winner

1 winner

Awarded to the best overall project of the hackathon.

Each team members will win a Nintendo Switch 2 and MLH winner pin

###### Beginner Track Winner

1 winner

Awarded to the best beginner project of the hackathon.

Each team member will win an iPad (11th Generation)and MLH winner pin
###### Google Cloud - Overall

1 winner

Ready to move beyond simple text chatbots? This track challenges you to build the next generation of AI applications using Google's most advanced tools for generative media and autonomous agents. We are looking for solutions that don't just talk, but do—creating rich media on the fly and executing complex tasks autonomously.

Whether you are focused on pushing the boundaries of creative expression with our latest media models or building sophisticated reasoning engines that can navigate real-world workflows, this is your chance to build with the tools that are defining the future of applied AI.

To qualify for the Google track's prizes, your project must be one of the following:

1\. Media Mastery: Projects that leverage Google's cutting-edge generative media models. This includes using Veo 3.1 for advanced video generation, Imagen 3 for high-fidelity creation, or Gemini 2.

results.title: AI ATL 24: Atlanta's Premier AI Hackathon - Devpost
excerpts:
From tackling real-world challenges to exploring the boundaries of what's possible, we're looking for projects that are useful, creative, and impactful. So, gather your team, brainstorm your ideas, and get ready to build the future with AI!

To qualify for the Google track’s prizes, your project must be one of the following:

1\. Most Useful: This track recognizes projects that address practical challenges with effective and user-friendly solutions.  
2\. Most Creative: This track celebrates projects that demonstrate innovative thinking, originality, and imaginative use of technology.  
3\. Most Impactful: This track highlights projects with the potential to create positive change, whether socially, environmentally, or economically.

###### Anthropic - Beginner

1 winner

Additional $250 in credits per person

Build with Claude by Anthropic

The official Anthropic track encourages teams to build any application type with the powerful and reliable Claude family of models.
We're excited to see cutting-edge deployments for coding, agents, social impact use cases, and more!

###### Anthropic - Overall

1 winner

Additional $250 in credits per person

Build with Claude by Anthropic

The official Anthropic track encourages teams to build any application type with the powerful and reliable Claude family of models. We're excited to see cutting-edge deployments for coding, agents, social impact use cases, and more!

###### NLX - Beginner

2 winners

Meta Quest 3 512GB (1 per team member)

Conversational applications are transforming the way people interact with technology in their daily lives and NLX’s patented multimodal technology is on the leading edge of this behavioral shift. In this track, experiment with this technology and build any conversational AI application that spans two or more modalities with the NLX platform. Modalities can be defined as voice, text, images, videos or physical such as IoT devices.

###### NLX - Overall

1 winner
Meta Quest 3 512GB (1 per team member)

Conversational applications are transforming the way people interact with technology in their daily lives and NLX’s patented multimodal technology is on the leading edge of this behavioral shift. In this track, experiment with this technology and build any conversational AI application that spans two or more modalities with the NLX platform. Modalities can be defined as voice, text, images, videos or physical such as IoT devices.

###### AI Alignment - AI Safety Initiative @ GT

2 winners

Presented by the AI safety initiative @ GT and proudly supported by Anthropic and Apart Research

We live in a world where new AI capabilities create both incredible opportunities and unimaginable threats. Knowing if a model is safe for a particular deployment, therefore, is the priority for legislators,, developers, and the common people.

results.title: Don’t Miss SoCalTech Fair 2025 – Gaming PC Deals, Influencers & More! | iBUYPOWER®
excerpts:
Jason Langevin, better known to his millions of followers as [JayzTwoCents](https://www.youtube.com/@Jayztwocents) , is launching a brand-new initiative called **SoCalTech** , a community-focused brand built to connect tech fans, creators, and businesses from across the region.

After more than a decade of creating content and connecting with tech lovers around the world, Jason is turning his focus toward giving back to his local community. “One thing I’ve learned over the last twelve years is that SoCal’s tech enthusiasts are some of the most passionate out there,” he says. “With SoCalTech, I want to help grow that passion into something bigger—something that brings people together in person.”

### The First-Ever SoCalTech Fair – May 3-4

To kick things off, **SoCalTech is hosting its inaugural event, the SoCalTech Fair** , happening May 3–4 at **iBUYPOWER’s headquarters** in Southern California.
Think of it as a cross between a tech expo and a classic county fair—packed with hands-on experiences, games, influencer meet-and-greets, and shopping all weekend long.

The event is being co-produced by JayzTwoCents, [iBUYPOWER](https://www.ibuypower.com/) , and [HYTE](https://hyte.com/) , two of the biggest names in [custom PC gaming](https://www.ibuypower.com/gaming-pcs/pc-builder) systems and components.

Here’s what you can expect at SoCalTech Fair:

**Free-to-play Gaming Stations** – Jump into the latest PC games or test your skills on sim-racing rigs.  
**Live Panels & Presentations** – Featuring JayzTwoCents, Austin Evans, BitWit, and more.  
**Tech Challenges & Competitions** – including a “Speed PC Building” and a “Tower of Power” motherboard stacking event.  
**Exclusive Sales** – Score discounted PC components, peripherals, and gear from iBUYPOWER and HYTE.  
**Event-Only Merch** – Limited edition swag and accessories available only at the fair.
### A Celebration of SoCal’s Tech Spirit

As a SoCal-based company, iBUYPOWER knows how strong and passionate the local tech scene is. “When Jay approached us with the idea for SoCalTech Fair, we were all in,” says Jeffrey Cheng, Senior Director of Sales and Marketing at iBUYPOWER. “This is about giving back to the same community that helped shape us 25 years ago.”

So whether you’re a lifelong PC builder, an aspiring content creator, or just someone who loves cool tech— **this is the event to check out.**

Learn more and get your tickets here: <https://www.ibuypower.com/community/socaltechfair>

Follow SoCalTech Fair updates on iBUYPOWER’s and JayzTwoCents’ social channels:

**iBUYPOWER** – [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/iBUYPOWERPC/) / [Twitter](https://x.com/iBUYPOWER) / [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/ibuypower/)

**JayzTwoCents** – [Twitter](https://x.com/JayzTwoCents) / [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/jayztwocents/) / [YouTube](https://www.youtube.
